claims.statement: The defined accessibility floor, including visible alternatives for runtime diagrams and charts, is checked before public release.
Public output identifies a final accountable non-AI person or institution with explicit rights-holder status and user confirmation; placeholders are rejected.

Carrier receipts persist, distinguish single-file output from carrier-plus-assets output, retain local-asset digests, and public release verifies rebuilt stable carrier fingerprints against an identified human browser/DOM review.
Coursewerk builds editable continuous, slide, and paged preview carriers from declared lean sources.
Direct edits calculate and gate the complete downstream consistency review set.
Every declared chapter has five structurally valid, mutually related deliverables.
Output roots use Git and accepted component hashes so direct edits remain visible.
Language polishing is an agent editorial workflow with human approval, not a deterministic QA result.
A chapter without a rights-cleared real photograph records an accountable, substantive media decision.
Every incorporated media item is traceable to structured provenance and public attribution, and verified external media retains hash-bound authoritative rights evidence.
Public packing refuses unresolved assurance, quality, source-comparison, key-fact critique, human carrier-review, or coherence failures and binds the accepted evidence to its receipt.

Every completed teaching package receives a hash-bound focused critique of identity, source/version, licenses, media rights, attribution, and chapter key facts; Light uses a context-reset same-model pass and Full uses cross-model review.

Public sources have hash-bound, known-policy-aware receipts separating verified copyright-license facts from access/AI-use notices; non-published source use remains advisory and public outputs enforce honest attribution and compatible licensing of copied or adapted expression.

Discovery release binds raw and extracted source evidence with structured retrieval metadata, rejects duplicate source identities/content, and retains an exact compact source revision record in the package.
Coursewerk repository changes are impact-indexed against their target branch merge base.
Coursewerk has a structured, accessible, dependency-free product website deployed from the repository through GitHub Pages.

The Coursewerk website presents authentic local screenshots and a clearly labelled link to a published AI-generated demonstration, while stating that it was built from revision-bound Wikipedia pages and is not intended for classroom use.

knownFindings.summary: Some accessibility-floor findings do not block release.
knownFindings.resolution: Alt text, diagram/chart text alternatives, heading starts/jumps/empties, and descriptive links are release-blocking; post-render behavior remains explicitly human-reviewed.

knownFindings.summary: Public output and original assets can identify an AI agent or temporary placeholder instead of an accountable rights holder.
knownFindings.resolution: Public foundations require a final named non-AI person/institution, explicit rights-holder status, and user confirmation; self-generated/user-owned creators must match an accountable author.

knownFindings.summary: Static carrier shells report a misleading zero rendered-image accessibility count.
knownFindings.resolution: Receipts label source and static-shell inspections precisely; release requires an identified human browser/DOM attestation bound to current sources, local assets, and stable carrier fingerprints.

knownFindings.summary: Media-bearing carriers are described as self-contained although local assets remain external.
knownFindings.resolution: Receipts classify single-file versus carrier-plus-assets modes, expose dependencies, and support strict --require-single-file enforcement; product wording was corrected.

knownFindings.summary: The carrier receipt hash cannot be reproduced because the exact receipt is not delivered.
knownFindings.resolution: Packing persists and hash-binds the canonical .carriers.json beside the release receipt.

knownFindings.summary: Runtime Mermaid/chart meaning can disappear offline without a visible alternative.
knownFindings.resolution: Public QA requires a substantive adjacent Visual description or Data summary for every Mermaid/chart block.

knownFindings.summary: Identical authoritative rights pages are stored repeatedly for several sources.
knownFindings.resolution: Rights capture content-address deduplicates identical evidence snapshots while preserving source-specific retrieval and preflight records.

knownFindings.summary: Detected process-notice excerpts contain raw HTML fragments.
knownFindings.resolution: Process scanning and excerpts operate on decoded visible text while preserving the raw snapshot separately.

knownFindings.summary: Rights snapshots omit retrieval and capture provenance needed to interpret the evidence.
knownFindings.resolution: Capture records mode, URLs/redirects, HTTP metadata, bytes, timestamp, tool version, and structured operator identity.

knownFindings.summary: Deliverable format checks are incomplete.
knownFindings.resolution: Chapter skeleton, objective propagation, slide templates, assessment sections, and practice counts are validated.

knownFindings.summary: Content and component index can be replaced together.
knownFindings.resolution: An external accepted-state ledger anchors the accepted component-index hash.

knownFindings.summary: General rights evidence and license text are only weakly asserted.
knownFindings.resolution: Hashed local evidence snapshots and exact official legal-text hashes are required.

knownFindings.summary: Light mode can omit independent examination of source/license facts and scientific key claims.
knownFindings.resolution: Every completed package requires a structured, hash-bound focused critique; Light uses a separate context-reset same-model pass and Full uses cross-model review, with at least one traced key fact per public chapter.

knownFindings.summary: Remote media can bypass provenance.
knownFindings.resolution: Markdown and HTML remote media are release-blocking; local assets require structured provenance.

knownFindings.summary: A chapter can silently omit the real-photo expectation in Coursewerk authoring guidance.
knownFindings.resolution: Each chapter either includes a rights-cleared photo or records a substantive accountable not-used decision in MEDIA_PLAN.json.

knownFindings.summary: A verified external media item can rely on an unretained license claim.
knownFindings.resolution: Public clearance requires an authoritative media description/license snapshot, hash, verification date, and structured retrieval/capture metadata; a capture helper creates the evidence.

knownFindings.summary: Regex syntax checks miss plugins and invalid nesting.
knownFindings.resolution: The real parser runs and generic plugin balance plus nested fence direction are enforced.

knownFindings.summary: Paged source routing conflicts with the package contract.
knownFindings.resolution: DOCUMENTS.json declares carrier type for sources in valid public/private/personal paths.

knownFindings.summary: A known public source can claim verified evidence when its expected notice is absent.
knownFindings.resolution: Known-source evidence expectations are enforced during capture and rechecked from a hash-bound receipt before public preparation; non-published use remains advisory.

knownFindings.summary: An AI-use notice can be overlooked or incorrectly presented as an amendment to a Creative Commons copyright license.
knownFindings.resolution: Capture preserves the exact notice, marks its legal effect undetermined, leaves non-published use open, and presents it as a nonblocking warning for an instructor considering publication.

knownFindings.summary: Provenance, usedIn, and attribution are not fully cross-validated.
knownFindings.resolution: Paths and usedIn are required, duplicates/orphans fail, and attribution is generated exactly.

knownFindings.summary: An authoring-stage QA report can show zero critical issues while final source/review gates are deferred.
knownFindings.resolution: Reports label authoring versus release runs explicitly and disclose deferred final gates; --for-discovery counts source, key-fact, visual, and coherence blockers.

knownFindings.summary: Extracted source text is hashed but the raw source and retrieval context are not digest-bound.
knownFindings.resolution: Automatic corpus entries include raw snapshot path/hash/byte count, structured retrieval metadata, canonical URL, versioned extractor, extracted-text hash/word count, and an exact compact revision record retained in the package.

knownFindings.summary: Source-rights readiness can conflate non-published authoring with public release.
knownFindings.resolution: Non-published source use is advisory and never requires a receipt; public-source evidence and release claims remain separately verified, while AI-use notices do not become copyright conclusions.

knownFindings.summary: Pack does not require carrier-build and evaluation receipts.
knownFindings.resolution: Packing reruns carriers and emits evaluation, archive hash, and structured release receipt.

knownFindings.summary: Report commands fail when the report parent directory does not exist.
knownFindings.resolution: Assurance and QA commands create report/JSON parent directories before writing.

knownFindings.summary: Scaffold input can falsely satisfy source comparison.
knownFindings.resolution: SOURCE_CORPUS binds primary IDs to hashed text or explicit attestation; scaffold paths are rejected.

knownFindings.summary: Exact source revision metadata is available only outside the released package.
knownFindings.resolution: Source preparation emits metadata/SOURCE_RECORD.json; QA requires an exact deterministic mirror and the release receipt hashes it.

knownFindings.summary: Wikipedia redirects or aliases can be counted as distinct source pages.
knownFindings.resolution: Preparation and public QA reject duplicate page IDs, revision IDs, and extracted-text hashes before authoring or release.

componentPolicy.identity: one stable repo:<path> component per indexed file
